00:24:25	Kamil Salakhiev:	https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/774
00:26:03	Edgar | ethrex:	we have a pr open implementing 8070 on ethrex
00:26:11	Bosul Mun:	Reacted to "we have a pr open im..." with 👍
00:26:28	Edgar | ethrex:	https://github.com/lambdaclass/ethrex/pull/6776
00:26:55	kasey:	[CL changes for testing] This would be to notify EL of custody group changes?
00:27:23	Kamil Salakhiev:	https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-metrics/pull/23
00:28:31	Daniel Knopik:	i have a branch, but i need to fix it x)
00:29:30	Bosul Mun:	Replying to "[CL changes for test..."

If you don’t use getBlobsV4, there could be more missing blobs, since if el doesn’t have enough cells to recover blobs on the blobpool then it will return nil for those
00:30:13	Bosul Mun:	Replying to "[CL changes for test..."

So ideally we want to have both engine apis on devnet
00:42:02	kasey:	Would the EL then fallback to “dense” blob pool if CL stops sending custody group metadata in FCU? That could be a nice operational feature, because then we could have a single flag in the CL, and that CL flag would drive both layers.
00:46:21	Csaba:	Why is is hasBlobs query, and not a hasBlob stream from from EL to CL?
00:47:22	Kamil Salakhiev:	do we have streaming support in engine api?
00:47:51	Csaba:	Replying to "do we have streaming..."

Don’t know, but I did propose to add it a few month ago :-)
00:48:17	Csaba:	Replying to "do we have streaming..."

AFAIK the protocol itself supports it
00:48:27	kasey:	A blob appearing where we didn’t have it seems fine, a blob disappearing would be a bigger issue.
00:48:43	Daniel Knopik:	I built hasBlobs support with the assumption that the received blobs might be different, both appearing and disappearing
00:48:47	Csaba:	Replying to "do we have streaming..."

I remember looking it up in JSON RPC, and there is something
00:49:31	Csaba:	Replying to "do we have streaming..."

That was my getBlobs “v3” improvement proposal before other v3 and v4 things came up
00:49:46	Daniel Knopik:	Replying to "I built hasBlobs s..."

this means e.g. that I do not implement optimization like intentionally not verifying incoming cells while waiting for getblobsv3
00:49:58	kasey:	FWIW, a lot of things can go sideways (buffering etc, across multiple implementations) with such a small streaming response. Meaning in practice we could very well not see the “fast” part of the message until we also get the “slow” part on the wire.
00:52:31	kasey:	Replying to "I built hasBlobs sup..."

My concern was more that if we tell peers that we have a particular blob, but we actually need it, seems more likely to run into bugs where they don’t send us the missing cells on bitmap update.
00:53:18	Daniel Knopik:	Replying to "I built hasBlobs s..."

i dont set it in my metadata until i truly have it

hasblobs only affects the request bitmap: i do not request promised blobs
00:56:31	kasey:	hasBlobs: I want
getBlobs: I have
00:56:37	Csaba:	Where is the hasBlobs description?
00:57:16	Kamil Salakhiev:	Replying to "Where is the hasBlob..."

We don’t have it yet. Marco should start working on that soon. But it is implemented in geth already
00:57:26	Csaba:	Replying to "Where is the hasBlob..."

OK
